sentences.en: Another Democratic socialist knocks out a moderate.
sentences.cn: 又一位民主社会主义者击败了一位温和派。
sentences.structure: 主语（Another Democratic socialist）+ 谓语（knocks out）+ 宾语（a moderate）
sentences.analysis: 简单句，knock out 意为“击败”，常用于选举语境。

sentences.en: A rare rebuke from the conservative court.
sentences.cn: 来自保守派法院的罕见批评。
sentences.structure: 名词短语：修饰语（A rare）+ 中心词（rebuke）+ 后置定语（from the conservative court）
sentences.analysis: 省略句，完整形式为“This is a rare rebuke...”，用于新闻标题。

sentences.en: They said the establishment and the oligarchy is just too big and too powerful to overcome.
sentences.cn: 他们说建制派和寡头集团太大太强，无法克服。
sentences.structure: 主语（They）+ 谓语（said）+ 宾语从句（the establishment...to overcome）
sentences.analysis: 宾语从句中使用“too...to...”结构，表示“太……而不能……”。

sentences.en: You are the proof that the power of organized people beats the power of organized money.
sentences.cn: 你们就是证明：组织起来的人民的力量胜过组织起来的金钱的力量。
sentences.structure: 主语（You）+ 系动词（are）+ 表语（the proof）+ 同位语从句（that...money）
sentences.analysis: 同位语从句解释proof的内容，对比organized people和organized money。

sentences.en: We believe that fundamental change can and will happen if we fight for it; if we organize and show no fear in standing up for what's right.
sentences.cn: 我们相信，如果我们为之奋斗，如果我们组织起来，无所畏惧地捍卫正确的事情，根本性的改变能够也必将发生。
sentences.structure: 主句（We believe）+ 宾语从句（that...right），从句中包含两个if条件状语从句
sentences.analysis: 并列条件句，强调行动与信念。

sentences.en: And what we're seeing with this series of wins, the ones you just outlined, but also were coming a week after Mamdani's sweep in New York.
sentences.cn: 我们看到的这一系列胜利，不仅是你刚才提到的那些，还有马姆达尼在纽约大获全胜一周后出现的。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What we're seeing）+ 系动词（are）+ 表语（this series of wins...），插入语（the ones you just outlined）
sentences.analysis: 主语从句作主语，插入语补充说明。

sentences.en: I do think the themes in these races speak to something broader in the Democratic base across the country.
sentences.cn: 我确实认为这些竞选中的主题与全国民主党基础选民中更广泛的东西产生了共鸣。
sentences.structure: 主句（I do think）+ 宾语从句（the themes...country）
sentences.analysis: do表示强调，speak to意为“对……有吸引力”。

sentences.en: You can talk boldly and loudly about all of these policies, but if you cannot govern, if you do not win, and losers do not legislate.
sentences.cn: 你可以大胆而响亮地谈论所有这些政策，但如果你不能治理，如果你不能获胜，而失败者不能立法。
sentences.structure: 并列句：第一分句（You can talk...policies）+ but连接第二分句（if...legislate）
sentences.analysis: 第二分句包含两个if条件句和一个并列句，强调行动胜于言辞。

sentences.en: And when you talk about the way that some of our more progressive candidates who are winning in deep-blue districts, the issues they are talking about are going to require them to have to compromise on what they believe if they want to respond to what they campaign.
sentences.cn: 当你谈到我们一些更进步的候选人在深蓝选区获胜的方式时，他们谈论的问题将要求他们必须在自己信仰的事情上妥协，如果他们想回应自己的竞选承诺。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句（When...districts）+ 主句（the issues...campaign），主句包含定语从句（they are talking about）和条件状语从句（if...campaign）
sentences.analysis: 多层嵌套，结构复杂，强调进步派面临的现实困境。

sentences.en: It's going to be interesting to see how these new, more radically left-wing candidates fare in terms of the upcoming midterms.
sentences.cn: 看看这些新的、更激进的左翼候选人在即将到来的中期选举中表现如何，将会很有趣。
sentences.structure: 形式主语（It）+ 系动词（is going to be）+ 表语（interesting）+ 真正主语（to see...midterms）
sentences.analysis: it作形式主语，不定式短语作真正主语。

sentences.en: And the first candidate that actually addresses these concerns in a real way is going to be the winner.
sentences.cn: 第一个真正以实际方式解决这些问题的候选人将成为赢家。
sentences.structure: 主语（The first candidate）+ 定语从句（that...way）+ 系动词（is going to be）+ 表语（the winner）
sentences.analysis: 定语从句修饰candidate，强调实际行动的重要性。

sentences.en: What the Supreme Court held is that the federal limits that go back to the 1970s on what are called coordinated expenditures, when a candidate coordinates their expenditures with the national party, those limits have been in place since 1976.
sentences.cn: 最高法院裁定，那些可追溯到20世纪70年代的联邦限制——关于所谓的协调支出，即候选人与全国政党协调支出——这些限制自1976年以来一直存在。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What the Supreme Court held）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语从句（that...1976），表语从句中包含定语从句（that go back to the 1970s）和插入语（when...party）
sentences.analysis: 结构复杂，包含多个从句，解释最高法院的裁决内容。

sentences.en: And the Supreme Court struck them down yesterday, even though, Audie, in 2001, the Supreme Court had specifically upheld them.
sentences.cn: 而最高法院昨天推翻了它们，尽管在2001年，最高法院曾明确维持过它们。
sentences.structure: 主句（And the Supreme Court struck them down yesterday）+ 让步状语从句（even though...upheld them）
sentences.analysis: even though引导让步状语，强调前后矛盾。

sentences.en: What I think that really does is it opens the spigot in this cycle, especially for Republicans.
sentences.cn: 我认为这真正的作用是，它在这个周期中打开了资金龙头，尤其是对共和党人而言。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What I think that really does）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语从句（it opens...Republicans）
sentences.analysis: 主语从句作主语，表语从句说明结果。

sentences.en: Last night's Colorado Democratic primary reveals 29-year-old Democratic socialist Melat Kiros defeated 15-term incumbent Diana DeGette.
sentences.cn: 昨晚的科罗拉多州民主党初选显示，29岁的民主社会主义者梅拉特·基罗斯击败了连任15届的现任议员戴安娜·德盖特。
sentences.structure: 主语（Last night's Colorado Democratic primary）+ 谓语（reveals）+ 宾语从句（29-year-old Democratic socialist Melat Kiros defeated 15-term incumbent Diana DeGette）
sentences.analysis: reveals后接省略that的宾语从句，从句主语为Melat Kiros，谓语defeated，宾语Diana DeGette。

sentences.en: Experience only matters if you actually do something with it.
sentences.cn: 经验只有当你真正用它做点什么时才有意义。
sentences.structure: 主语（Experience）+ 谓语（matters）+ 条件状语从句（if you actually do something with it）
sentences.analysis: if引导条件状语从句，主句为Experience only matters。

sentences.en: We have 71 districts on our map, 45 where we're on offense that we think we can flip.
sentences.cn: 我们的地图上有71个选区，其中45个是我们认为可以翻转的进攻选区。
sentences.structure: 主句（We have 71 districts on our map）+ 同位语（45 where we're on offense that we think we can flip）
sentences.analysis: 45为71 districts的同位语，where引导定语从句修饰45，that引导定语从句修饰offense（或理解为we think的宾语从句）。

sentences.en: And in these swing districts across the country, we have incredible candidates running because they are connected to their communities and focused on really appealing across their communities.
sentences.cn: 在全国各地的这些摇摆选区，我们有出色的候选人参选，因为他们与社区联系紧密，并专注于真正吸引整个社区。
sentences.structure: 地点状语（And in these swing districts across the country）+ 主句（we have incredible candidates running）+ 原因状语从句（because they are connected to their communities and focused on really appealing across their communities）
sentences.analysis: running为现在分词作后置定语修饰candidates；because引导原因状语从句，从句主语they，并列谓语are connected和focused。

sentences.en: We actually won last cycle, in 2024, 14 districts that Donald Trump also won.
sentences.cn: 实际上，在上一个选举周期（2024年），我们赢得了14个唐纳德·特朗普也赢得的选区。
sentences.structure: 主语（We）+ 谓语（won）+ 宾语（14 districts）+ 定语从句（that Donald Trump also won）+ 时间状语（last cycle, in 2024）
sentences.analysis: that引导定语从句修饰districts，从句主语Donald Trump，谓语won。

sentences.en: And if you are someone in the Democratic establishment, you should be deeply concerned about what this says about where the party is, what the standing of the party is, what the ability of the party infrastructure is to actually win elections.
sentences.cn: 如果你是民主党内部人士，你应该深切关注这说明了什么：党的现状、党的地位、以及党的基础设施实际赢得选举的能力。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（if you are someone in the Democratic establishment）+ 主句（you should be deeply concerned about）+ 宾语从句（what this says about...）
sentences.analysis: about后接三个并列的what引导的宾语从句：what this says about where the party is, what the standing of the party is, what the ability...is。

sentences.en: The real test for some of these candidates who do not consider themselves a part of the Democratic traditional infrastructure is going to be how they continue to galvanize or energize these voters for us to win up and down the ballot in November.
sentences.cn: 对于这些不认为自己是民主党传统基础设施一部分的候选人来说，真正的考验将是他们如何继续激励或动员这些选民，以便我们在11月的各级选举中获胜。
sentences.structure: 主语（The real test for some of these candidates）+ 定语从句（who do not consider themselves a part of the Democratic traditional infrastructure）+ 系动词（is going to be）+ 表语从句（how they continue to galvanize or energize these voters for us to win up and down the ballot in November）
sentences.analysis: who引导定语从句修饰candidates；how引导表语从句，从句中for us to win...为不定式短语作目的状语。

sentences.en: The Democratic Party, on economics, their messaging used to be so good, but then you had this horrible four years with Joe Biden where people saw 9.5 percent inflation.
sentences.cn: 民主党在经济方面的信息传递曾经非常好，但随后你经历了与乔·拜登一起的可怕四年，人们看到了9.5%的通胀率。
sentences.structure: 主语（The Democratic Party）+ 插入语（on economics）+ 主句（their messaging used to be so good）+ 转折并列句（but then you had this horrible four years with Joe Biden）+ 定语从句（where people saw 9.5 percent inflation）
sentences.analysis: where引导定语从句修饰four years，从句主语people，谓语saw。

sentences.en: And every one of these socialists that's running, they're going to have to force all the people in the purple districts and swing districts to answer to them.
sentences.cn: 而这些正在参选的社会主义者，他们将不得不迫使所有紫色选区和摇摆选区的人向他们交代。
sentences.structure: 主语（every one of these socialists）+ 定语从句（that's running）+ 主句（they're going to have to force all the people in the purple districts and swing districts to answer to them）
sentences.analysis: that's running为定语从句修饰socialists；force sb. to do sth.结构。

sentences.en: You cannot run away from the fact that more Americans are paying more for gas, goods, groceries, services and health care, including the 18.5 million Americans in places that Donald Trump won who have seen their premiums, co-pays and deductibles rise because of your lack of action.
sentences.cn: 你无法逃避这样一个事实：更多的美国人在汽油、商品、食品杂货、服务和医疗保健上支付更多，包括在唐纳德·特朗普赢得的地区的1850万美国人，他们因为你们的不作为而看到自己的保费、共付额和免赔额上涨。
sentences.structure: 主句（You cannot run away from the fact）+ 同位语从句（that more Americans are paying more for...）+ 分词短语（including the 18.5 million Americans）+ 定语从句（in places that Donald Trump won）+ 定语从句（who have seen their premiums...rise because of your lack of action）
sentences.analysis: that引导同位语从句说明fact；including短语包含两个定语从句：that Donald Trump won修饰places，who have seen...修饰Americans。

topics.title: Democratic Party Primaries and Progressive vs. Establishment Divide
topics.content: 2026年科罗拉多州民主党初选中，进步派候选人梅拉特·基罗斯击败了任职近30年的现任议员戴安娜·德盖特，另一位进步派菲尔·韦泽击败了参议员迈克尔·贝内特。这反映了民主党内部进步派与建制派之间的持续斗争，以及选民对现状的不满。
topics.keywords: Democratic primary, progressive, establishment, Colorado, Melat Kiros, Phil Weiser

topics.title: Birthright Citizenship and Supreme Court Ruling
topics.content: 美国最高法院以6比3裁定特朗普总统试图通过行政命令终止出生公民权的行为违宪，但5比4的多数意见认为国会可能有权修改相关法律。这引发了关于移民政策和宪法第十四修正案的辩论。
topics.keywords: birthright citizenship, Supreme Court, executive order, 14th Amendment, immigration

topics.title: Transgender Athletes Bans and Supreme Court Decision
topics.content: 最高法院维持了西弗吉尼亚州和爱达荷州等州禁止跨性别运动员参加女子体育项目的禁令，认为这不违反联邦反歧视法。该裁决将问题交回各州，预计会引发进一步的法律和政治斗争。
topics.keywords: transgender athletes, Supreme Court, Title IX, state bans, sports

topics.title: Campaign Finance and Supreme Court Ruling on Coordinated Expenditures
topics.content: 最高法院推翻了自1976年以来对候选人与全国政党协调支出的联邦限制，这被视为自公民联合案以来最重要的竞选资金裁决，预计将大幅增加政治资金流动，尤其有利于共和党。
topics.keywords: campaign finance, coordinated expenditures, Supreme Court, Citizens United, political spending

topics.title: LeBron James Free Agency
topics.content: 勒布朗·詹姆斯离开洛杉矶湖人队成为自由球员，引发关于他可能重返克利夫兰或其他球队的猜测。球迷反应不一，部分湖人球迷认为他的加盟未达预期。
topics.keywords: LeBron James, free agent, Lakers, NBA, Cleveland

topics.title: 美国民主党内部派系斗争与初选趋势
topics.content: 2026年科罗拉多州民主党初选中，29岁的民主社会主义者梅拉特·基罗斯击败了连任15届的现任议员戴安娜·德盖特，反映了民主党内部进步派与传统建制派的矛盾。类似情况在纽约、洛杉矶等地也出现，民主社会主义者赢得多个初选。这引发了对民主党未来方向、选举策略以及如何整合不同派系能量的讨论。
topics.keywords: Democratic primary, socialist, incumbent, faction, progressive, establishment

topics.title: 美国中期选举策略与摇摆选区
topics.content: 民主党国会竞选委员会主席苏珊·德尔贝尼强调，赢得众议院多数席位的关键在于紫色选区和摇摆选区，而非深蓝选区。她指出民主党在2024年赢得了14个特朗普赢得的选区，并计划在2026年翻转更多共和党席位。同时，共和党在竞选资金上领先，但民主党认为其候选人与社区联系更紧密。
topics.keywords: midterm election, purple district, swing district, majority, campaign finance, DCCC, NRCC

topics.title: 特朗普政府的大规模赦免计划
topics.content: 据《大西洋月刊》报道，特朗普政府正考虑在美国250周年之际赦免250人，名单包括马来西亚逃犯刘特佐、说唱歌手Pras等争议人物，甚至涉及外国实体。批评者认为这一过程缺乏透明度，存在腐败风险，与传统的基于功绩的赦免程序不同。
topics.keywords: presidential pardon, clemency, Trump administration, corruption, lobbyist, The Atlantic

topics.title: 国会立法僵局与共和党内部分歧
topics.content: 众议院因共和党强硬派阻挠而无法通过关键法案，包括SAVE法案（投票监管法案）。参议院共和党人也表示缺乏通过该法案的票数。民主党指责共和党内部混乱，导致国会效率低下，而共和党则归咎于参议院。
topics.keywords: Congress, legislative gridlock, SAVE Act, Republican civil war, House, Senate
